title: Spectral Unitarity for Hodge — Can Degeneration Force Surjectivity?

analysis.object: T739: Spectral Unitarity for Hodge — Can Degeneration Force Surjectivity? depth analysis

hodge_spectral_sequence.description: Hodge spectral sequence E_1^{p,q} = H^q(X,Omega^p) => H^{p+q}(X,C)

e1_degeneration.description: For Kähler X: spectral sequence degenerates at E_1

degeneration_and_hodge.description: E_1 degeneration forces Hodge decomposition H^n = direct sum H^{p,n-p}

unitarity_condition.description: Weil operator C: acts on H^{p,q} by i^{p-q} -- unitary

unitarity_forces_surjectivity.description: If unitary action on Hodge classes extends to algebraic cycles, surjectivity follows
unitarity_forces_surjectivity.reason: Unitarity does not force algebraic representability

spectral_gap.description: Spectral degeneration is EML-2; Hodge surjectivity is EML-inf -- different levels

t739_conclusion.description: Spectral unitarity is a DESCRIPTION of Hodge classes, not a SOURCE of algebraic cycles -- T739
t739_conclusion.reason: Approach blocked; spectral methods are insufficient

analysis.theorem: T739: Spectral Unitarity for Hodge — Can Degeneration Force Surjectivity? (S1018).

key_theorem: T739: Spectral Unitarity for Hodge — Can Degeneration Force Surjectivity? (S1018).
